what_it_does: This file is a structured design ledger that documents the visual grammar, narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, and generative design rules for a 15-page ConceptV investor/sales pitch deck.
observations: Contains a 15-entry attention_flow list mapping each slide p-01 through p-15 to a reading-order description.

Catalogs roughly 40 design findings grouped into categories such as color-palette, gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, line-connector, atom, zone, chrome-mode, sequence, and texture.
Each finding follows a repeated schema: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent boolean, and generator_rule.
Uses strong imperative language in generator_rule fields, e.g. 'Apply the gold exclusively to...', 'Hexagon is RESERVED...', 'Never use...'.
Maps the slide sequence to a rhetorical arc: FEEL → UNDERSTAND THE PROBLEM → SEE THE SOLUTION → TRUST THE TEAM → UNDERSTAND THE PATH → KNOW THE ECONOMICS.
Treats repeated slide templates (p-05/p-06, p-10/p-11, p-14/p-15) as an intentional scalability argument rather than redundancy.
The file ends mid-sentence inside the SAM/market-size box recipe, so it is truncated.
Contains no actual image assets, code symbols, or explicit schema version; it is purely a textual design substrate.
standouts: Template reuse as narrative rhetoric — structural repetition is framed as the scalability argument.
Hexagon reserved for the Virtual Hub node — unique geometry encodes the central platform concept.
Strict gold/khaki accent system with role separation: numbers, sub-headings, active states, and bullets only.
Diagonal hatch texture used as a domain signal referencing architectural drafting paper.
Progressive reveal by minimal delta: p-05 to p-06 adds one separator line to redirect attention without rebuilding the layout.
conventions: Follows a tokenized, atomic design-system documentation convention with categories like atoms, zones, and chrome modes.
Maintains a neutral, file-local, descriptive tone without ranking or prescribing winners.
Uses a consistent findings schema with recipe/evidence/emergent/generator_rule fields.
Breaks typical JSON brevity by embedding long prose descriptions, pixel approximations, and imperative rules.
Explicitly defines exceptions, such as page titles using black only to make gold accents more potent.
Uses an emergent boolean to distinguish derived rules from foundational ones, though the criterion is not defined.
concerns: The file is truncated mid-value, leaving the last SAM/market-size box recipe incomplete.
Color values and pixel measurements are approximate ('approximately', 'appears to be') with no stated source or calibration method.
Generator rules are written as imperatives, which may blur the line between observation and prescription in a neutral ledger.
There is no explicit schema or version for findings, making programmatic parsing fragile.
Evidence references slide IDs (p-01..p-15) that are not otherwise defined or linked to assets in this file.
The emergent flag is applied subjectively without a documented definition.
notes: A dense, rule-based design autopsy of a 15-slide pitch deck, truncated near the end, that treats slide templates and visual atoms as reusable rhetorical code.
questions: Is there a companion image or PDF file containing the actual deck visuals?
How were the approximate color values, pixel sizes, and measurements derived?
What is the intended consumer of the generator_rule fields — human designers, code generators, or LLM prompts?
How is the emergent flag determined and used during downstream fusion?
Are the referenced 1-page and 30-page versions stored elsewhere in the repository?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a neutral, file-local design ledger for the 15-page pitch deck; the actual content matches that, but the file is truncated before finishing the final finding.
apparent_themes: Strict semantic color and geometry coding
intention_signals: It tries to turn a pitch deck into a reproducible design grammar: every visual choice is paired with a rule so the deck can be regenerated, extended to other verticals, and kept visually and rhetorically consistent.
novelty: The combination of fine-grained attention-flow mapping, explicit generative rules, and the framing of repeated slide templates as an argument about scalability is unusual for a static design extract.
contribution: A structured, rule-rich description of the deck's visual language, narrative arc, attention flow, and atomic components that a fused system can use to reconstruct, extend, or compare the 15-page ConceptV pitch deck format.
summary_for_embedding: A structured design ledger documenting the visual grammar, narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, and generative rules for the 15-page ConceptV investor pitch deck.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the deck's design DNA as reusable, rule-based substrate for consistent regeneration and extension across formats and verticals.
